business.headline: Does your credit score matter when applying for a bank job?You may think your credit score only matters for loans, but could it also affect your chances of getting a bank job? Let's find out.

Sensex rallies over 1,100 points in a strong start to FY27The BSE Sensex rose 1,186.77 points, or 1.65%, to close at 73,134.32. The Nifty 50 gained 348 points, or 1.56%, to settle at 22,679.40, holding firmly above the 22,600 mark.

Blinkit brings quick commerce inside Mumbai Airport terminalsThe service allows travellers to order essentials directly through the Blinkit app and have them delivered anywhere within the departure area, including boarding gates, lounges, food courts, and select partner outlets.

Brent crude oil slips below $99 as Trump hints at possible end to conflictThe effective closure of the Strait has already started to impact global markets. Reduced supply has pushed energy prices higher and raised fears of inflation.

Is India's LPG crisis over or does a new threat loom amid Iran war?There are early signs of possible de-escalation in the West Asia war, but experts say that the current situation should be seen as 'temporary relief, not a full all-clear', even as supply conditions have improved.

Paying over Rs 50,000 rent? Don't ignore this 2% TDS ruleIf your rent crosses Rs 50,000 a month, there's more to do than just paying your landlord on time. A key TDS rule applies, and missing it could lead to extra costs.

From LPG to ATM charges: 7 changes hitting your everyday finances from todayYour daily expenses may not stay the same this year. Starting today, changes in LPG prices, ATM usage rules and more are set to impact how you manage your money.

Jet fuel price hiked: Will your flight tickets cost more?With ATF prices rising and global oil markets volatile, how will the hike impact domestic and international flight travel in the days ahead? Read on to find out.

From HRA to gift vouchers: 9 big income tax changes affecting you from April 1A new financial year often brings fresh rules, and this time is no different. Starting April 1, multiple income tax changes, from allowances to workplace perks, are set to influence your day-to-day finances. Let's have a look.

Garden Reach shares jump 18%: Why GRSE stock price rose today?GRSE's strong financial results boost investor confidence and lift shipbuilding stocks. GRSE reported a record annual turnover of Rs 6,400 crore for FY26, compared to Rs 5,076 crore in the previous financial year, marking a 26% rise.

Oracle's layoffs explained: Why the IT giant suddenly fired 30,000 employeesIndia appears to be among the worst-hit markets, with reports indicating that nearly 12,000 employees may have been laid off out of a workforce of around 30,000.

IndiGo shares jump 9%: Why are aviation stocks rising today?IndiGo shares climbed as much as 8.33% to Rs 4272.20, while SpiceJet rose over 4.4% to Rs 10.17 around 9:48 am, signalling continued buying interest in the sector.

Sensex closes 1,187 points higher: Why the stock market rose today?Today's surge is being powered by hopes of easing tensions, cheaper oil and a bounce in oversold stocks, but investors will be watching if the rally has real legs.

Sensex, Nifty see sharp rally on hopes of Iran war de-escalation; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex rallied 1,868.41 points to 73,815.96, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 576.45 points to 22,907.85 as of 9:26 am.

Will rupee slide to 100 against the US dollar as Iran war drags on?Rupee has declined by around 10% over the past year and is among the worst-performing currencies in Asia this year. Analysts say that while authorities are trying to slow the fall, these steps may only provide short-term support.

Jet fuel prices rise; government staggers hike to shield domestic faresATF prices have been revised upwards, but government intervention has capped the impact on domestic airlines, even as uncertainty persists for international fares.

India's tobacco trap: Can jobs and public health be balanced?Although India has committed internationally through the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (FCTC) to reduce tobacco-related harm, policy decisions sometimes contradict this.

Commercial LPG price hiked by Rs 195.5, mini cylinder rates up by Rs 51In Delhi, the price of a 19-kg commercial LPG cylinder has been increased by Rs 195.50, bringing the new retail cost to Rs 2,078.50.

Thinking of foreclosing your loan? Here's what it does to your credit scoreGot some extra cash and thinking of closing your loan early? It may sound like a smart move, but many borrowers still wonder — what does it do to your credit score?

Will flight ticket prices rise from April?Air travel may get a bit more expensive from April as fuel prices are expected to rise. With airlines facing higher operating costs, will passengers soon have to pay more for tickets?

entertainment.headline: Mammootty, Mohanlal's Patriot new release date announced. Details inside The Malayalam film Patriot, starring Mammootty and Mohanlal, has been postponed and will now release on a fresh date. The makers assured fans that the delay was due to unforeseen reasons and promised a high-quality cinematic experience.

Megan Thee Stallion hospitalised after mid-show exit during Broadway performance Rapper and singer Megan Thee Stallion was rushed to hospital after falling ill during a Broadway performance of Moulin Rouge! The Musical in New York. Due to the health scare, she was forced to make an abrupt exit before the show had concluded.

The Pitt's Laetitia Hollard on what went behind the sensitive rape kit episode The Pitt Season 2 featured a deeply sensitive episode on sexual assault. Actor Laetitia Hollard highlighted the extensive preparation and empathy involved in portraying trauma-informed care.

Zendaya plans to disappear after packed 2026 slate, fears fans may get sick of her Hollywood star Zendaya is set to dominate 2026 with a series of thrilling projects. After this bustling year, she plans to take a brief break from the limelight, promising a return in 2027.

Ranbir Kapoor fan praises his 'Dhurandhar performance'. Here's how he responded A fan's playful Dhurandhar comment to Ranbir Kapoor at the Ramayana teaser launch sparked a witty response. Here's what happened next.

Priyanka Chopra remembers her father during Golden Temple visit, shares pics Priyanka Chopra took a serene break from her busy schedule to visit the Golden Temple in Amritsar. She also performed seva at the gurdwara.

Aakhri Sawal: First look of Sanjay Dutt's film about RSS out on Hanuman Jayanti The teaser of Sanjay Dutt's upcoming film Aakhri Sawal will be released nationwide on April 2, on the occasion of Hanuman Jayanti. The film is based on the 100-year journey of RSS (Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh).

Priyadarshan's 100th film starring Mohanlal to go on floors in December | Exclusive Priyadarshan has teamed up with actor Mohanlal for his 100th directorial venture. We have learnt that the story will explore the emotional beats of a father-son relationship.

For a moment, it felt like Aslam: Pak cop's wife praises Sanjay Dutt for Dhurandhar Naureen Aslam reacted to Dhurandhar's portrayal of her late husband, praising Sanjay Dutt's performance but questioning key aspects of the film. She pointed out what the film got right, and where she felt it missed the mark on Chaudhry Aslam's real story.

Dhurandhar 2: Ahida Sarmai's small role as Major Iqbal's daughter adds big emotion Ahida Sarmai's brief role in Dhurandhar 2 added a heartfelt emotional layer, humanising Arjun Rampal's Major Iqbal. Her presence also highlighted the importance of inclusivity in Bollywood storytelling.

Kangana Ranaut returning as Rani in Queen 2, shoot to start soon: Report Actor Kangana Ranaut is set to return as Rani in Queen 2, with reports suggesting the sequel may begin filming soon. Here's what we know so far.

Suits actor Patrick Adams spills playful on-set secret about co-star Meghan Markle Fans of Suits will love this playful behind-the-scenes anecdote from Patrick J Adams, who recalls Meghan Markle's hilarious eye-rolls on set. The actor reflects on their off-screen camaraderie and talks about his cheeky Instagram bio.

No Dunesday? Avengers: Doomsday eyes earlier December release, say reports Marvel Studios is reportedly considering adjusting the Avengers: Doomsday release date to secure more IMAX screens. The shift could avoid direct competition with Dune: Part Three in December 2026.
Nayanthara teams up with Salman Khan for Vamshi Paidipally's SVC63 Riding on her pan-India success, Nayanthara joins Salman Khan's SVC63, adding further weight to the film's star-driven lineup.

Priyanka Chopra calls Dhurandhar a revelation, congratulates 'dost' Ranveer Singh Priyanka Chopra Jonas appreciated her Gunday co-star Ranveer Singh for the mega success of his film Dhurandhar: The Revenge. She also addressed director Aditya Dhar for his storytelling in a post on Instagram.

Parineeti Chopra supports Raghav Chadha's call for legal paternity leave Parineeti Chopra supported Raghav Chadha's call for legal paternity leave in Parliament.  This sparked a wider conversation on shared parenting in India.

Salman was so sweet, never found it difficult to work with Akshaye: Priyadarshan Veteran filmmaker Priyadarshan opened up about his experience of working with Salman Khan and Akshaye Khanna, dismissing their "difficult" reputations. He is now gearing up for his upcoming film Bhooth Bangla with Akshay Kumar, marking their reunion after 14 years.

Fan gifts Ranbir Kapoor a Ram Mandir vest, says he sees Arun Govil in actor Ranbir Kapoor's portrayal of Lord Rama sparked comparisons to Arun Govil. A fan gifted him a Ram Mandir-inspired vest at a special screening at Los Angeles. Here's what happened.

Malayalam director Ranjith remanded in judicial custody in sexual assault case Malayalam filmmaker Ranjith Balakrishnan has been remanded in 14-day judicial custody following his arrest in a sexual assault case. A young female actor accused him of sexual misconduct inside a caravan in January.

general news.headline: Iran asking for truce: Trump vows to keep blasting Tehran until Hormuz is freeDonald Trump said Washington would only consider a ceasefire if the Strait of Hormuz is reopened and remains "open, free, and clear", linking any pause in hostilities to maritime access along the crucial oil route.

IRGC now Iran's de facto ruler amid rift with PresidentWith the whereabouts of Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei not clear, the powerful IRGC has further consolidated its grip over Iran. It has even sidelined the Iranian president, taking vital and sensitive leadership calls.

Oracle's layoffs explained: Why the IT giant suddenly fired 30,000 employeesIndia appears to be among the worst-hit markets, with reports indicating that nearly 12,000 employees may have been laid off out of a workforce of around 30,000.

Irony of Iran war: India to get oil from Tehran first time in 7 yearsA crude oil tanker is headed for India with oil from Iran, a first since May 2019. The war has allowed India to buy oil and gas from Russia and Iran, both sanctioned by the US.

Iran has the Trump card, Trump has the JokerIran is waging an economic war as it fights the military might of the US and Israel. Because causing damage to the US economy is where it will hurt Washington the most.

LSG vs DC Live: Delhi opt to bowl, no place for Mayank and NabiLucknow (LSG) vs Delhi (DC) Live Score, IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals have won the toss and opted to field. No Auqib Nabi for the visiting side as they open the batting with Pathum Nissanka and KL Rahul. No Mayank Yadav for LSG, but Mohammed Shami to make his debut for the home team in Lucknow today.
You're beautiful, your daughter must be too, cop tells accused woman; suspendedA Maharashtra police officer allegedly asked a woman accused to send her daughter to his home in exchange for money.

Oracle lays off 12,000 in India, here is the severance employees say they gotOracle layoffs have reportedly impacted as many as 30,000 employees across regions. In India, about 12,000 Oracle employees, maybe in line for the dreaded pink slip. Here is how much these laid off employees are likely to get as part of their severance package from Oracle.

Why FCRA Bill triggered a storm, had to he held back | Explained in 5 pointsThe Centre's proposed changes to the FCRA law have been put on hold for now, following protests from Opposition parties and Kerala church groups. They criticised the Bill, saying it would allow the Centre to take control of minority assets built with foreign donations.

Explosion outside Punjab BJP office in Chandigarh, cops probing cause of blastThe blast occurred in the vicinity of the BJP office, with investigation agencies now trying to ascertain the cause of the explosion and assess the extent of damage.

From 'India Out' to India's help: Quiet shift in neighbourhood amid Iran warThe Iran war and the subsequent energy crisis have severely exposed the vulnerabilities of energy-importing countries like Bangladesh, the Maldives and Sri Lanka. The same countries witnessed an anti-India sentiment during varying periods. However, India, leaving the bitterness behind, has extended a helping hand.

Not enough money: Adam Zampa dissatisfied with IPL pay, explains move to PSLAdam Zampa skipped the IPL 2026, citing dissatisfaction with the pay relative to his skills and the league's two-month schedule. Zampa is currently playing for Karachi Kings in PSL 2026.
Biju Patnaik: The fighter pilot who defied several empires

IAS officer who did sit-ups on 1st day of posting resigns, says he was sidelinedIn his resignation letter, Rinku Singh Rahi, a 2022-batch IAS officer of the Uttar Pradesh cadre, has alleged that he was sidelined and not given adequate opportunities to work actively.

How mangoes are fighting and losing to climate changeIn this week's Climate on My Plate, let's look at the king of fruits, what it needs to grow and how climate change is damaging it, and with it many livelihoods and the spirit of summer.

From Mughals to Orry: CBSE Class 12 History paper QR code goes off syllabusCBSE's exam papers are serving more than marks this season: they are serving memes too. After the Class 12 Maths paper went viral for a QR code that led to Rick Astley's "Never Gonna Give You Up", the Class 12 History paper on March 30, has now pushed internet personality Orry into the same classroom spotlight!

Pak politician Nabil Gabol reacts to portrayal of Dawood Ibrahim in Dhurandhar 2Dhurandhar: The Revenge has sparked real-world chatter. Pakistani politician Nabil Gabol has reacted to a scene featuring Dawood Ibrahim in the film.

What will Trump do next? World awaits US President's address with bated breathTrump is set to address the nation tonight, outlining US's next course of action in the Iran conflict. Amid a deepening global oil crisis, world leaders and markets alike are watching closely. With the Strait of Hormuz remaining disrupted, Trump's next move could have far-reaching consequences for energy markets and global stability. As uncertainty lingers, the stakes extend far beyond the battlefield.

Bihar equation shifts after Rajya Sabha polls, 3 Congress MLAs vote for BJPAt the centre of the political churn in Bihar is cross-voting by three Congress MLAs, who backed the BJP candidate despite the party holding six legislators in the state.
Video: Woman flung into air after being hit by speeding car in MangaluruA woman was seriously injured after she was hit by a speeding car while crossing the road in Bantwal taluk of Mangaluru.

Why shouting at your child in public can have lasting effectsWere you a little harsh with your child when they were being extra demanding at a restaurant? Or did you shout at them when they weren't listening to you at the mall? It might be time to pause and reflect.

Live: Trump says US to exit Iran pretty quickly and return if neededUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: As the Iran-US-Israel conflict intensifies, multiple flashpoints are emerging across the region, signalling a dangerous escalation. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said President Donald Trump is open to asking Middle Eastern allies to help finance ongoing military operations against Iran, even as Tehran insists it will not step back without compensation. Meanwhile, thousands of US Army troops from the elite 82nd Airborne Division have begun arriving in the region, deepening Washington's military footprint.  Iranian sources also claimed a Mahan Air civilian plane bound for New Delhi on a humanitarian medicines mission was struck at Mashhad airport. In Tehran, Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, in a fourth written statement, hailed Revolutionary Guard Navy chief Alireza Tangsiri as a brave symbol of Iran's Gulf presence. He was killed last week in a strike. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest and live updates.

Not just stress, this is burnout. Can mindfulness fix India's hustle culture?Stress and burnout are not the same, but many are treating them alike. From exam pressure to workplace fatigue, emotional strain is rising across India. Experts say recognising burnout early and using mindfulness, discipline, and awareness can help students and professionals cope better and protect long-term mental wellbeing.

Four astronauts to launch to the Moon tonight: All about Artemis-IINasa's Artemis-II mission launches tonight at 3:54 am IST, sending four astronauts farther from Earth than any human since Apollo 17 in 1972.

sports.headline: Arjun Tendulkar doesn't dream of playing for India IPL 2026: Arjun Tendulkar focuses on personal growth and a fresh start with Lucknow Super Giants, while voicing his opposition to the IPL's Impact Player rule.

Verstappen retirement talk, Bearmann crash lead F1's review into new regulations Following Oliver Bearman's crash at the Japanese Grand Prix, Formula 1's governing body, the FIA, has announced a series of meetings in April to review the new 2026 regulations. With races in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia called off, the governing body will use the unexpected break to reassess the rules. The decision also comes amid growing pressure after Max Verstappen hinted at a possible retirement.

LSG vs DC Live: Auqib Nabi to debut? Mayank Yadav bowling full-tilt Lucknow (LSG) vs Delhi (DC) Live Score, IPL 2026: Toss to take place at 7 PM IST. Mayank Yadav, who has been riddled with injuries in the past few years, is showing positive signs ahead of the start of the season. The question remains if the pacer can stay fit all through the season this time.

Shubman Gill criticised for poor captaincy in GT's loss to Punjab IPL: Aakash Chopra criticised Gujarat Titans' tactical approach after their loss to Punjab Kings. Chopra questioned two key captaincy decisions made by Shubman Gill in the match, which had direct links to Gujarat's loss against Punjab in Mullanpur.

Iraq qualify for FIFA World Cup after 40 years with 2-1 win over Bolivia Iraq national football team sealed a historic return to the FIFA World Cup after 40 years, defeating Bolivia national football team in the intercontinental playoffs.

From DPL drama to IPL smiles: Nitish Rana and Digvesh Rathi patch up Nitish Rana and Digvesh Rathi appeared to put their DPL 2025 feud behind them after sharing a light-hearted moment ahead of the IPL 2026 clash between Delhi Capitals and Lucknow Super Giants in Lucknow.

India to play T20I series in Zimbabwe 4 days after finishing England tour The Indian Cricket Board has announced a 3-match tour of Zimbabwe in the month of July. The Indian team will be playing in Africa, four days after completing their tour of England.

In Lucknow, Rishabh Pant searches for his perfect Galauti balance Rishabh Pant begins IPL 2026 in Lucknow leaner and more focused. He is seeking a Galauti-like balance between flair and control as he looks to rediscover consistency and reclaim his place in India's white-ball plans.

FIFA refuses to shift Iran's World Cup matches despite Donald Trump's threat FIFA has refused to shift Iran's World Cup matches out of the United States. Gianni Infantino has stood his ground that the schedule will remain unchanged despite Donald Trump hinting at threats to Iranian players.

India Women's record broken as Amelia Kerr leads New Zealand's historic ODI chase NZW vs SAW, 2nd ODI: Amelia Kerr scored her fifth ODI century as New Zealand beat South Africa by two wickets in Wellington on Wednesday, completing the highest successful run chase in Women's ODI history.

Not enough money: Adam Zampa dissatisfied with IPL pay, explains move to PSL Adam Zampa skipped the IPL 2026, citing dissatisfaction with the pay relative to his skills and the league's two-month schedule. Zampa is currently playing for Karachi Kings in PSL 2026.

Shreyas Iyer in trouble? Yuzvedra Chahal updates on Punjab skipper's injury scareIPL 2026, PBKS vs GT: Yuzvendra Chahal shared an update on Shreyas Iyer, who suffered an injury scare during Punjab Kings' clash against Gujarat Titans on Tuesday in Mullanpur.

Tiger Woods steps away from golf after DUI accident controversy, to seek helpTiger Woods has decided to step away from golf to focus on his health after pleading not guilty to DUI charges following a car crash in Florida. He has ruled himself out of the upcoming Augusta Masters to seek treatment.

Shreyas Iyer penalised in Punjab Kings' handsome IPL 2026 startIPL 2026, PBKS vs GT: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer has been fined Rs 12 lakh for a slow over-rate offence during PBKS' first match against Gujarat Titans. Iyer scored 18 off 11 as Punjab beat Gujarat by three wickets in Mullanpur.

Candidates: Hyper-aggressive Pragg suffers major setback, slips to 3rd spotCandidates Tournament: Indian Grand Master R Praggnanandhaa slipped to third place at the FIDE Candidates tournament after a loss to Javokhir Sindarov. Pragg faltered while trying to be aggressive against the 20-year-old Uzbekistani.

End of Maxwell's Australia career? All-rounder snubbed from central contractVeteran all-rounder Glenn Maxwell has been omitted from Cricket Australia's central contract. This raises fresh questions over whether the 37-year-old still fits into the team's long-term plans in international cricket.

Inglis who? No BBL tax for Connolly as project Punjab finds a new No.3 generalCooper Connolly showed that he is one Big Bash League talent who can excel in the IPL, making the No.3 spot at PBKS his own after Josh Inglis' exit from the franchise. With lessons from Punjab legend Shaun Marsh, the all-rounder could prove to be a key player this season for the Kings.

Emotional Lionel Messi breaks down in tears during final match in Argentina: WatchLionel Messi was left in tears during what could be his final match on Argentine soil, before delivering a goal and an assist in Argentina's emotional 5-0 win over Zambia in Buenos Aires.

IPL 2026 Play of the Day: Prasidh Krishna's skiddy burst jolts PBKS in MullanpurPBKS vs GT, IPL 2026 Play of The Day: Prasidh Krishna's skiddy spell turned a comfortable Punjab Kings chase into a tense contest, using bounce and variation after rain to trigger a collapse and briefly hand Gujarat Titans control.

IPL: Do Delhi Capitals have what it takes to end their title drought?IPL 2026, Delhi Capitals preview: Among the three teams still chasing a first IPL title, Delhi Capitals appear better equipped than ever, with a well-rounded squad that blends international experience and domestic depth. Yet, their familiar struggles with inconsistency and injuries could once again define how far they go.

Azzurri Apocalypse 3.0: Italy miss FIFA World Cup months after cricket joyIn a surreal sporting paradox, Italy's recent ICC T20 World Cup heroics have been eclipsed by a footballing apocalypse: for the third consecutive time, the four-time world champions have failed to qualify for the FIFA World Cup, falling to a crushing penalty shootout defeat against Bosnia and Herzegovina in Zenica.

Preview: After a season of what-ifs, LSG and DC chase opening punch in LucknowIPL 2026, LSG vs DC preview: After campaigns of squandered promise, Lucknow Super Giants and Delhi Capitals reset in Lucknow, boasting stronger, more balanced squads. The northern neighbours look to seize early momentum and finally turn potential into consistent results.

Living the IPL dream: Dushmantha Chameera aims to step up for Delhi CapitalsDushmantha Chameera calls IPL a dream and aims to step up for Delhi Capitals. The Sri Lankan pacer highlights mental strength as key to success in batting-friendly conditions ahead of the clash against LSG.

Shreyas Iyer cautious after injury scare in Mullanpur thriller: Don't want to jinx itShreyas Iyer remained cautious after a wrist injury during PBKS' win, saying he "doesn't want to jinx it". His knock was cut short, but debutant Cooper Connolly's 72 guided Punjab Kings to a tense victory over Gujarat Titans.

Kevin Pietersen praises Mumbai's 'outstanding' AQI, blasts paid promotion claimsFormer England captain Kevin Pietersen has sparked a social media debate after praising Mumbai's air quality as the "best" he has experienced, while also hitting back at users who accused him of doing paid promotion.

Cooper Connolly impresses on debut as PBKS outsmart GT in nervy winIPL 2026, PBKS vs GT: Australia's Cooper Connolly marked his IPL debut with a decisive 44-ball 72 as PBKS held their nerve to seal a three-wicket win over Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026's first thriller.

Williams scores on debut as India beat Hong Kong in Asian Cup 2027 QualifiersRyan Williams scored on debut as India beat Hong Kong 2-1 in Kochi to finish their AFC Asian Cup qualifying campaign with a win. Akash Mishra also got on the scoresheet as India ended their campaign with five points.

Tottenham appoint Roberto De Zerbi in desperate bid to stave off relegationTottenham Hotspur have turned to Roberto De Zerbi as a last-ditch attempt to salvage their Premier League status, appointing the Italian as their third manager of a turbulent campaign.

Watch: PSL ground staff nearly swept away after heavy wind in LahorePSL 2026: Ground staff were nearly swept away after strong winds lashed Lahore ahead of the clash between Islamabad United and Peshawar Zalmi at Gaddafi Stadium, with heavy rain forcing a delay in the toss.

Napoli-Romelu Lukaku feud: Italian giants planning to freeze out Belgian strikerNapoli confirm Romelu Lukaku missed training and face disciplinary action. Club considers suspension, contract implications as striker cites injury concerns.

Days after IPL withdrawal, Ben Duckett fined for speeding in EnglandDays after pulling out of the Indian Premier League, England Test opener Ben Duckett has been fined £700 after being caught speeding at 93mph while on his way to a game in his home country.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
